page: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores using Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system features 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes a massive local storage capacity of 15200 GB delivered via NVMe SSDs, and a high-speed network interface with a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. The system excels in memory bandwidth, achieving top 25% ratings in both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang reach top 10% performance tiers. Single-core performance is moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hpc6id.32xlarge offers high resource density by combining dedicated physical cores, a large memory footprint, and extensive local NVMe storage. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable compute performance for parallel workloads, though it limits concurrent thread execution compared to hyperthreaded alternatives. The massive 15.2 TB local storage and 200 Gbps network bandwidth reduce data transfer bottlenecks, making it a highly integrated platform for data-intensive tasks.

This server is designed for high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data decompression, software compilation, and memory-intensive simulation workloads. The combination of high-speed local storage and high network throughput also supports distributed file systems and data-intensive ETL pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 high-performance computing instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 15200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, all running on the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, placing in the top 25%, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation reach the top 10% tier. Single-core performance remains in the middle 50%. The lack of hyperthreading ensures dedicated execution resources per core, making this instance highly efficient for parallel computing. It is suited for high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and data-intensive workloads requiring massive local storage and high network throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge features 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 1024 GB RAM, 15.2 TB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-threaded and compilation performance.

tagline: A dedicated 64-core Intel Xeon platform featuring 15.2 TB of local NVMe storage and 200 Gbps networking for demanding parallel workloads.

1024 GB of DDR4 memory providing 16 GB per core
15200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor

Optimized for high-performance computing, databases, and parallel simulations
